title: epilobium hirsutum subsp. grandiflorum
intro: Epilobium hirsutum subsp. grandiflorum, also known as the hairy willowherb, is a plant species in the willowherb family (Onagraceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting urinary tract health. Herbal preparations of Epilobium hirsutum subsp. grandiflorum, such as infusions and tinctures, have been used to treat conditions like urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and even certain types of cancer. Additionally, the plant's antioxidant properties may help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being.
